description_long: This bollard is a tall, slender, and vertically ribbed post with classical architectural detailing. The shaft is fluted, reminiscent of Greco-Roman columns, and is interrupted at its midpoint by a decorative ring or collar. The base is slightly flared and rounded, providing stability and a subtle ornamental touch. The top features a square cap with additional decorative molding, giving it a formal and stately appearance. The overall color is a light grayish-white, suggesting it is made from painted or coated metal, possibly cast iron or steel, and designed to blend with traditional or historic urban environments.
description: A tall, fluted, classical-style bollard with a decorative ring at the center, a flared base, and a square cap, finished in light gray.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full height, fluted shaft, central decorative ring, and the square cap clearly.
description_view_1: Side view is nearly identical to the front, with the fluting and decorative features still visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with the same fluted shaft, central ring, and cap details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the symmetrical fluting and decorative elements.
